body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes â€” aider has a within-turn reflection loop

The scenario asks for an agent that fails a tool call, interprets the failure, and retries with corrected input across multiple cycles *within one turn* (no intervening user prompt). Aider implements exactly this via its reflection loop. `Coder.run_one` (aider/coders/base_coder.py:924) is a `while message:` loop: it calls `send_message`, and if `self.reflected_message` is set it loops again with that failure text as the next model input, up to `max_reflections = 3` (base_coder.py:100, 939-944). Reflections are seeded by auto-lint failures ("Attempt to fix lint errors?" â†’ `reflected_message = lint_errors`, base_coder.py:1603-1607) and auto-test failures ("Attempt to fix test errors?" â†’ `reflected_message = test_errors`, base_coder.py:1619-1623), and shell-command output is fed back into the conversation as a user/assistant pair (base_coder.py:1609-1614). All of this happens under a SINGLE user prompt: `io.user_input(...)` â€” which writes the `#### ` history header â€” is called exactly once per turn, in `run`/`run_stream` BEFORE `run_one` (base_coder.py:860, 879). The reflection iterations inside `run_one` call `send_message` directly and never re-emit a `#### ` header, so the whole failâ†’retryâ†’recover arc is one logical turn from the transcript's point of view.

### daemon_capability: full â€” the parser emits the contiguous working span the spec requires

The canonical user-observable signal is: state stays continuously `working` across all retry cycles, with no flicker to `ready` or `waiting` between attempts, ending in `ready`. The aider adapter is built to emit exactly this. Its `Source` is `FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", RawLineParser}` (agent.go:45-50). The parser opens a turn on the `#### ` line (`turnOpen = true`) and, critically, treats every `> Tokens: â€¦` line as `assistant_message` â€” NOT `turn_done` â€” deliberately keeping the turn open across multiple model calls (parser.go:146-188; the doc comment at parser.go:18-23 calls out that multiple `> Tokens:` lines within one `####` are normal). Tool calls land as `> Running <cmd>` â†’ `tool_result(Bash)` and `> Applied edit to` â†’ `tool_result(Edit)` (parser.go:67-70, 119-124, 231-242), and a `tool_result` never changes session state away from `working`. So the topology the spec asserts â€” one contiguous `working` span spanning N tool-call/result pairs and N model calls, then a single `working â†’ ready` at turn end â€” is precisely what the parser is built to emit, and the classifier holds `working` across the multi-call reflection run within one `#### ` turn. No spec-required observable is mis-handled: a trace exists in the watched Source and the parser handles it correctly today. Hence `full` (the old `partial` is split here â€” the only residual concern is a non-spec timing window, captured below as a caveat, not a downgrade).

### driver_capability: ready â€” recipe needs only send + wait_turn

The recipe drives one `send` (prompt aider to create `solution.py`), then waits TWICE on `wait_turn` (first `> Tokens:` = initial edit; second = first reflection round), then a trailing `sleep` to absorb the IdleFlush settle. The interactive driver (scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh) implements `send`, `wait_turn`, and `sleep` â€” every step type this recipe needs. No gapped primitive (restart/reset_session/resume/start_session/exit_clean/sigkill/keys/session/fork) is exercised. Hence `ready`.

### The residual concern is a non-spec timing window, not a daemon bug

Aider's idle TUI prompt never lands in the markdown history, so the parser has NO in-band end-of-turn signal; it synthesizes `turn_done` via `IdleFlush` after the transcript has been quiet for `aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter = 1500ms` (parser.go:54, 208-229). Between two reflection cycles the only thing happening is a model call, and the transcript is silent for the whole duration of that call. If any single inter-reflection model call exceeds ~1.5s of file silence, `IdleFlush` *can* fire a premature `turn_done` (working â†’ ready) and the next `> Tokens:` line in the same logical turn reopens it (ready â†’ working) â€” a flicker the scenario forbids. This is the matrix note's "idle-flush splits attempts" concern. It is NOT a `bug`: the daemon does not mis-handle a spec-required observable â€” it correctly emits the contiguous span whenever each model call lands inside the window, and the recipe arms the reflection loop deterministically (`--auto-test --test-cmd false`) so the within-turn retry is exercised. The flicker is a probabilistic, latency-dependent artifact of an out-of-band turn-boundary heuristic, which is exactly what a caveat is for. Per the #476 authoring rule (caveats over downgrades; narrow NON-spec gap keeps `full`), it stays `daemon_capability: full` with the timing window noted as a caveat. A fast model finishes each call inside the window and the span is clean.

### Primitive / capability anchor

The primitive is `tool_calls` + iterative within-turn retry. The capability key is `tool_calls`, which is `true` in replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json. The within-turn-retry aspect has no dedicated capability flag â€” it is a property of the classifier holding `working` across `> Tokens:`/`tool_result` runs, not a declared agent feature, so no capabilities.json correction is warranted.

## Matrix comparison

The coverage row reads `agent_supports: yes`, `daemon_capability: unknown`, `driver_capability: ready`, note "aider retries across turns rather than within; idle-flush splits attempts." This re-assessment resolves the `unknown` daemon axis to `full` and ratifies `driver_capability: ready`. Two refinements for the maintainer: (1) the source shows aider's reflection loop is genuinely *within* a single turn (one `#### ` header, `run_one`'s `while message:` loop), so the "retries across turns" half of the note is imprecise; (2) the idle-flush flicker is a non-spec timing caveat, not a daemon bug â€” the daemon emits the required contiguous span correctly.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant adjacent: aider has no in-band end-of-turn marker (the idle TUI prompt is not written to the markdown history), so turn boundaries are inferred purely from a 1500ms idle-flush window (parser.go IdleFlush).

Non-spec timing window: if an inter-reflection model call leaves the transcript silent for longer than aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter (1500ms), IdleFlush can synthesize a premature turn_done (workingâ†’ready) and the next reflection's `> Tokens:` line reopens the turn (readyâ†’working). Fast local models that finish each call inside the window avoid this; slow remote models can trip it. This is a probabilistic latency artifact of the out-of-band heuristic, not a mis-handled spec observable â€” hence a caveat, not daemon_capability: bug.

sources.note: FilesUnderCWD markdown parser: `> Tokens:` â†’ assistant_message (turn stays open across multiple model calls, parser.go:18-23,146-188); `> Running`/`Applied edit` â†’ tool_result (never changes state); turn_done only via IdleFlush (aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter=1500ms, parser.go:54,208-229) or LLM-error blockquote â€” this is why daemon_capability is full (contiguous working span emitted) with the idle-flush window as a caveat
Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} (agent.go:45-50); per-process Parser instance tracks idle-flush turnOpen state

aider 0.86.2: run_one() while-loop reflection (lines 924-944, max_reflections=3); lint/test failures set reflected_message (1599-1623); io.user_input (the #### header) called once per turn before run_one (run/run_stream, lines 860/879) â€” confirms the retry arc is one logical turn

user_input() writes the `#### ` prefix to .aider.chat.history.md (lines 775-789); only invoked for the top-level prompt, not for reflected messages â€” confirms reflections do not re-open a turn in the irrlicht parser
tool_calls: true, headless_mode: true (the two `requires` for this scenario); no within-turn-retry feature flag exists (it is a classifier property, so no correction warranted)
interactive driver implements send + wait_turn + sleep â€” every step type the recipe needs (one send, two wait_turn, one sleep); no gapped primitive exercised â†’ driver_capability: ready
